display.purpose: Hero headlines and the strongest page statement only.

headline.purpose: Major documentation sections and page-level section breaks.

title.purpose: Cards, examples, troubleshooting entries, and smaller content groups.

body.purpose: Long prose, descriptions, notes, and docs explanation.

label.purpose: Command flags, nav labels, terminal captions, metadata, and compact controls.

shadows.name: command-glow
shadows.purpose: Soft atmospheric halo around command blocks and signature terminal surfaces.

shadows.name: feature-hover-glow
shadows.purpose: Hover-only glow for feature cards and signature public-site moments.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default state transitions for color, border, opacity, and transform.

motion.name: entrance-rise
motion.purpose: Section and hero entrance motion, always with content present by default.

components.name: Primary CTA Button
components.description: Use for the main public-site action when the reader should move forward.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #d4a853; color: #0b1d0b; border: 2px solid #d4a853; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px 32px; font-family: Source Serif 4, Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; transition: background-color 200ms ease-out, color 200ms ease-out, transform 200ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgba(212,168,83,0.55); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Outline CTA Button
components.description: Use for secondary public-site actions and docs links that need presence without stealing focus.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; background: transparent; color: #d4a853; border: 2px solid #d4a853; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px 32px; font-family: Source Serif 4, Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; transition: background-color 200ms ease-out, color 200ms ease-out, transform 200ms ease-out; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: #d4a853; color: #0b1d0b; transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgba(212,168,83,0.55); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Copy Button
components.description: Compact mono control used inside command blocks.
components.css: .ds-copy-button { min-height: 44px; background: #1a3a1a; color: #f5e6d3; border: 1px solid rgba(212,168,83,0.30); border-radius: 4px; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.875rem; transition: background-color 200ms ease-out, color 200ms ease-out; } .ds-copy-button:hover { background: #1a3a1a; color: #d4a853; } .ds-copy-button:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgba(212,168,83,0.55); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Command Block
components.description: Signature terminal-style component for runnable commands.
components.css: .ds-command { position: relative; overflow: hidden; background: rgba(26,58,26,0.90); color: #e8f0e8; border: 1px solid rgba(212,168,83,0.20); border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 0 18px rgba(212,168,83,0.20); font-family: JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, monospace; } .ds-command-bar { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 12px; padding: 8px 16px; border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(212,168,83,0.20); background: rgba(11,29,11,0.50); color: rgba(245,230,211,0.60); font-size: 0.75rem; } .ds-command-copy { background: #1a3a1a; color: #f5e6d3; border: 1px solid rgba(212,168,83,0.30); border-radius: 4px; padding: 4px 12px; font: inherit; } .ds-command-body { display: flex; gap: 8px; padding: 16px; font-size: 0.875rem; } .ds-command-prompt { color: #d4a853; } .ds-command code { color: #e8f0e8; white-space: pre-wrap; overflow-wrap: anywhere; }

components.name: Docs Card
components.description: Readable documentation grouping for examples, workflows, safety notes, and troubleshooting.
components.html:
Create a task worktree

Use this when the work deserves a branch, review, and normal Git history.
components.css: .ds-docs-card { background: rgba(26,58,26,0.25); color: #e8f0e8; border: 1px solid rgba(212,168,83,0.20); border-radius: 8px; padding: 24px; } .ds-docs-card h3 { margin: 0 0 8px; font-family: Cinzel, Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.35rem; line-height: 1.18; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.005em; } .ds-docs-card p { margin: 0; max-width: 62ch; color: rgba(245,230,211,0.70); font-family: Source Serif 4, Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; line-height: 1.65; }

components.name: Docs Nav Link
components.description: Quiet left-rail navigation link for long documentation pages.
components.css: .ds-docs-nav { display: block; padding: 4px 0; color: rgba(245,230,211,0.70); font-family: Source Serif 4, Georgia, serif; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.5; text-decoration: none; transition: color 160ms ease-out; } .ds-docs-nav:hover, .ds-docs-nav:focus-visible { color: #d4a853; outline: none; }

components.name: Mobile Docs Trigger
components.description: Compact mobile entrypoint into the docs table of contents.
components.css: .ds-mobile-docs { min-height: 44px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px; background: transparent; color: #f5e6d3; border: 1px solid rgba(212,168,83,0.20); border-radius: 8px; padding: 6px 12px; font-family: JetBrains Mono, ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 0.75rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.04em; transition: background-color 160ms ease-out, color 160ms ease-out; } .ds-mobile-docs:hover { background: rgba(26,58,26,0.60); color: #d4a853; } .ds-mobile-docs:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid rgba(212,168,83,0.55); outline-offset: 2px; }

narrative.overview:
The Yggdrasil Worktree system feels like a command manual found in a mythic forest workshop: dark, quiet, tactile, and built for repeated use. It gives the public site a distinct identity without making command examples feel theatrical. The tree metaphor is present in color, naming, and atmosphere, while the layout stays practical enough for developers who came to solve a workflow problem.

This system rejects generic SaaS polish, terminal costume, and decorative mythology. Surfaces should feel grown from the same root: deep forest backgrounds, mist-green panels, rare gold emphasis, serif-led voice, and mono command details. Documentation pages can be expressive, but dense references must behave like tools.
keyCharacteristics: Dark forest surfaces with gold used as a scarce signal.
Serif display and body type, with mono reserved for commands, labels, and control text.
Tonal layering, borders, and soft glows instead of heavy app shadows.
Practical command examples placed before exhaustive reference detail.
Motion that reveals structure without delaying access to content.

rules.name: The Scarce Rune Rule
rules.body: Gold is the reader's signal color. If every border, heading, and icon is gold, the system loses hierarchy.

rules.name: The Forest Before Purple Rule
rules.body: Purple supports atmosphere through glows and gradients. It is never the dominant surface, text, or navigation color.

rules.name: The Mono Means Work Rule
rules.body: Mono is reserved for command text, flags, labels, and controls. Do not use it as a lazy shorthand for developer personality.

rules.name: The Serif Carries Trust Rule
rules.body: Long-form docs use Source Serif 4 with generous line-height. Do not collapse docs prose into cramped UI sans patterns.

rules.name: The Tonal Layer Rule
rules.body: A panel should become visible through green surface shifts and gold borders before it uses shadow.

rules.name: The Glow Must Earn It Rule
rules.body: Glow belongs to terminal, hero, and signature feature moments. It is prohibited as generic card decoration.

dos: Do teach the workflow before the flag list.
Do make safety visible whenever a command can move, copy, apply, or delete work.
Do use Deep Forest, Mist Green, and Parchment as the reading base, with Gold Rune as the scarce signal.
Do keep command examples concrete and runnable.
Do use the Yggdrasil metaphor to add identity, not ambiguity.
donts: Don't create generic SaaS docs pages with interchangeable cards, soft gradients, and vague productivity promises.
Don't use terminal-only hacker aesthetics that make the product feel narrower than the workflow it supports.
Don't add decorative mythology that obscures the actual Git and worktree behavior.
Don't write dense command references that present completeness before the reader has a working mental model.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent on cards, list items, callouts, or alerts.
Don't pair a 1px border with a soft wide drop shadow on the same card. Use a defined border or a restrained glow, not both as decoration.
